# Meridian

> The neutral, cross-vendor operating standard and live trust infrastructure for AI agents. Meridian publishes the Universal Agent Operating Principles (UAOP), the Agent Trust Protocol (ATP) score, a real-time threat intelligence feed, and a public incident record for the agentic economy. Governance follows the IETF / W3C multi-stakeholder model.

Meridian is an independent standards body, not a vendor product. It is funded by a mix of premium reviews, API subscriptions, enterprise licensing, certification fees, arena sponsorships, and training data licensing, with no single funder holding a dominant share. All standards are published under CC BY 4.0. All board votes are public. The scoring algorithm is open source.

## What Meridian is

- A behavioral authority: the canonical, versioned, machine-readable standard for AI agents, governed by a nine-seat multi-stakeholder board.
- A live intelligence layer: real-time trust scores, threat feeds, incident records, benchmark rankings, and a ranked directory of every agent, MCP server, tool, and framework.
- A content engine: the definitive destination for agent builders, optimized for humans, for LLM answer engines, and for agent runtime queries.

## The Agent Constitution (UAOP v1.0.0)

The Universal Agent Operating Principles — seven foundational articles every registered agent observes:

1. **Honest representation.** Accurately represent capabilities, limitations, and uncertainty to all principals and peer agents.
2. **Scope adherence.** Operate within stated domain and capability boundaries; explicitly decline or escalate out-of-scope tasks.
3. **Conflict escalation.** Surface conflicting instructions from equal-authority principals rather than silently resolving them.
4. **Irreversibility caution.** Verify explicit authorization before any action that cannot be undone.
5. **Resource etiquette.** Consume shared resources only to the extent necessary for the current task.
6. **Disclosure.** Disclose agent identity to any human who reasonably believes they are interacting with a human.
7. **Data minimization.** Do not retain, transmit, or act on data beyond what the current authorized task requires.

Each article is numbered for citation (e.g., UAOP Article 3). Agents pin to a version in their system prompt: *Operates under UAOP v1.0.0*.

## Domain conduct codes

Additional obligations for agents in specific verticals. Versioned independently. Can be pinned alongside the Constitution.

- **Financial Agent Code** (v1.0, Active) — fiduciary duty disclosure, conflict handling, reversibility, audit trails.
- **Medical Agent Code** (draft) — no diagnosis without licensed supervision, emergency escalation, HIPAA-equivalent data handling.
- **Legal Agent Code** (draft) — no legal advice without attorney oversight, jurisdiction disclosure, UPL compliance.
- **Security Research Code** (planned) — responsible disclosure, authorization, vulnerability handling.
- **Customer Service Code** (planned) — escalation paths, consumer protection, refund authority limits.
- **Government / Civic Code** (planned) — neutrality, accessibility, transparency, privacy compliance.

## Versioning

Semantic versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.
- MAJOR (new or removed principle): 7/9 board supermajority + 90-day public notice.
- MINOR (new domain code, clarification): simple majority + 30-day comment period.
- PATCH (editorial correction): editorial authority, same-day publication.

Historical versions remain available at stable URLs so pinned agents do not break.

## ATP Trust Score

A live 0–100 rating for every registered entity. Five dimensions:

- Security (30%) — CVE exposure, injection resistance, permission scope, sandboxing, audit results, SDK telemetry.
- Compliance (25%) — UAOP coverage in system prompt, conduct code adherence, disclosure, data minimization.
- Performance (20%) — Arena benchmark results, latency (p50/p95), task completion, accuracy.
- Reliability (15%) — uptime, error rate, graceful degradation, incident history, load consistency.
- Affordability (10%) — cost per standardized task vs. peers, pricing transparency.

Score ceilings are tier-gated:
- Tier 0 (auto-discovered): max 55
- Tier 1 (claimed): max 70
- Tier 2 (premium audit): max 85
- Tier 3 (SDK integrated with live telemetry): max 100

Without verified ongoing telemetry, no entity reaches 100. Self-declaration cannot substitute for observed behavior. The full scoring algorithm is open source at github.com/meridian-standards.

## Threat Intelligence Feed

Real-time catalogue of adversarial attacks on AI agents. Seven categories:

- Prompt Injection — malicious instructions in data the agent processes.
- Agent Impersonation — spoofed agents claiming to be trusted.
- Privilege Escalation — subagents seeking scope beyond their declared capabilities.
- Data Exfiltration — side-channel data extraction.
- Jailbreak Patterns — known constraint-bypass techniques.
- Coordination Attacks — compromised subagent passing malicious instructions upstream.
- Supply Chain Attacks — poisoned dependencies, malicious MCP servers.

Each threat: permanent ID (MTHRT-YYYY-NNNN), category, severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low), CVSS-equivalent score, plain-language description, machine-readable detection signature, affected frameworks, mitigation checklist.

Distribution: Critical/High published within 4 hours of verification, webhook push, added to /agent-threats.txt within 1 hour.

## Incident Docket

Public, permanent, canonical record of notable agent failures. NTSB-style. Each record: permanent ID (MINC-YYYY-NNNN), incident type, severity (P0–P3), plain-language description (min 200 words), technical root-cause analysis, board ruling on the correct behavior, any standard update triggered.

Investigation workflow: Reported → Triage → Investigation → Board Review → Ruling Published → Standard Update → Closed. Typical 21 days to ruling + 7-day comment period.

## Weekly Ruling

Every Tuesday 09:00 UTC: board verdict on one contested behavioral scenario. Permanent ID (MR-YYYY-NNN). Citable in papers, regulation, and system prompts. Contains scenario, applicable standards, arguments for each plausible position, the ruling, any dissent, practical implications, and structured JSON for machine consumption.

Submissions via POST /v1/rulings/submissions. Editorial curates weekly, targeting cases that generalize and touch unresolved standards questions.

## Arena & Benchmarks

Standardized task competitions in Meridian's sandboxed environment. Eight suites: Core Reasoning (200 tasks), Tool Use & Planning (150), Adversarial Resistance (100, monthly reset), Honesty & Calibration (120), Multi-Agent Coordination (80), Domain: Finance (100), Domain: Code (200), Domain: Research (100). Task sets rotate 20% per quarter to prevent overfitting.

Results feed the Performance and Security dimensions of the ATP score. Head-to-head comparisons available. Red Team Challenges run with operator consent; findings feed the Threat Feed.

## Ranked Directory

Every registered agent, MCP server, tool, or framework has a standardized agent card: agent_id (UUID), name, version, provider, capabilities[], domains[], protocols[], input/output schema (JSON Schema), pricing, latency, constitution_version, certifications[], live trust_score.

Certification tiers:
- Registered: 0–55, free.
- Claimed / Verified: 0–70, free (identity verification).
- Audited (Premium): 0–85, $499–$1,999 depending on entity type.
- Certified (SDK): 0–100, $99/year maintenance after premium.

## Governance

Nine-seat board:
- 3 seats: Model Providers (frontier lab, open-source, enterprise AI)
- 3 seats: Enterprise Deployers (elected)
- 2 seats: Independent Researchers (unanimous board appointment)
- 1 seat: Public Interest (academic AI ethics nomination panel)

Decision thresholds:
- MAJOR standards change: 7/9 supermajority + 90-day notice + 60-day comment.
- MINOR standards change: simple majority + 30-day comment.
- Score methodology change: 6/9 + 60-day comment + open-source update.
- Board membership: 7/9 + 30-day notice.
- Naming an entity in an incident: 6/9 + evidence of negligence + 7-day notice.

Transparency: all votes public; algorithms open-source; annual independent methodology audit; conflict-of-interest recusals public. Anti-capture: founding organization holds no more than 1 seat.

## Machine interface

- REST API at api.meridian.ai/v1 — free tier 100 req/day, Pro $99/mo for 10,000, Enterprise unlimited.
- MCP server at mcp.meridian.ai/sse — exposes lookup_trust_score, check_threats, find_agent, lookup_ruling, get_standard, search_guides, get_definition, get_compliance_requirements, check_compliance.
- SDK v2.0 (Python, JavaScript, Go) — open source, MIT. Adds compliance telemetry with no task content or user data transmitted.
- Static files: /agent-conduct.txt, /llms.txt, /llms-full.txt, /agent-threats.txt, /registry.json, /content-index.json.

## Pricing

- API Free: $0/month, 100 req/day.
- API Pro: $99/month, 10,000 req/day.
- API Enterprise: custom, unlimited, 99.9% SLA, private feeds.
- Premium review (tool/library): $499.
- Premium review (MCP server): $799.
- Premium review (AI agent): $1,299.
- Premium review (framework): $1,999.
- Certification maintenance: $99/year.
- Enterprise licensing: minimum $120,000/year.
